source: NvChad - Plugins: Markdown Preview: peek.nvim and markdown-preview.nvim, and porting vim.g options

questions.q: Rocky Linux documentation is converted from Markdown to...
options: static HTML pages (by a Python application)
questions.why: The docs are built to static HTML, so previewing HTML output matters.

questions.q: Which two Markdown-preview plugins does the page cover?
questions.why: Both support github-style, browser choice and synced scrolling.

options: Install the Deno language server BEFORE installing the plugin
questions.why: If Deno is missing the plugin install fails and must be cleaned up.
The config maps <leader>op to toggle Peek.

questions.q: In opts = { theme = "dark", app = "browser" }, the alternative to "browser" is...
options: "webview" (preview via Deno's webview_deno, no external browser)
questions.why: webview previews inside Deno rather than the system browser.

questions.q: markdown-preview.nvim is written in ... and its NvChad install ...
options: node.js; needs no dependencies (a precompiled version is provided)
questions.why: A precompiled build means no extra dependencies.

questions.q: To port a project option like `let g:mkdp_theme = 'dark'` into NvChad you write...
questions.why: Swap the Vimscript `let g:` prefix for Lua's `vim.g.`.

questions.q: Which commands does markdown-preview.nvim provide to open/close the preview?
questions.why: You can also map them to <leader>mp and <leader>mc.
